# Copilot instructions — CTIMS repository

Purpose: provide repository-specific signals for Copilot sessions to make accurate, small-scope edits and suggestions.

1) Build, test, and lint commands
- No CI/workflow files, test framework, or linter config were detected in the repository root.
- No explicit build, test, or lint commands were found in project files. If you add one, put canonical commands into this section (examples: `pytest tests/`, `flake8 .`, `python -m unittest tests`), so Copilot can reference them.

2) High-level architecture
- app/ contains the web application code organized by blueprints:
  - app/blueprints/{admin,api,auth,main} — blueprint packages for feature areas.
  - app/extensions — extension initializers (bcrypt, db, migrate, login_manager, console) used across the app.
  - app/models — ORM models.
  - app/forms — WTForms or similar form classes.
  - app/static and app/templates — per-blueprint static assets and templates.
- conf/ is present but currently contains no files; intended for deploy/runtime configuration.
- manage.py and wsgi.py are present as typical entry points; at the moment they appear to be placeholders.

3) Key conventions (repo-specific)
- Blueprint-first layout: put blueprint package modules under app/blueprints/<name>/ and add templates/static namespaced by that blueprint.
- Extension modules live in app/extensions/<name>.py and expose the initialized object for import (e.g., from app.extensions.db import db).
- Models are placed in app/models and imported by blueprint modules when needed.
- Keep one place for application wiring (app/__init__.py) — current file is empty; future blueprints and extension registration should go here.
- Static files are organized per-blueprint under app/static/<blueprint>/ to keep assets scoped.

4) Files and places to check when making changes
- app/blueprints/* for route and view changes.
- app/extensions/ when adding or modifying global services (DB, login manager, bcrypt, migrations).
- wsgi.py and manage.py for deployment/CLI changes.
- conf/ for runtime configuration (added files should be noted here).

5) AI assistant/Other assistant configs checked
- No COPILOT-specific instruction file existed prior to this addition.
- No CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, AGENTS.md, .windsurfrules, CONVENTIONS.md, AIDER_CONVENTIONS.md, .clinerules or similar AI assistant configuration files were detected.

Notes for Copilot sessions
- Prefer small, localized edits: modify a single blueprint or extension module rather than broad cross-cutting changes.
- If introducing tests or CI, add the exact commands and configuration files to the repo root so Copilot can reference them next time.
